title: xanthium echinatum subsp. italicum
intro: Xanthium echinatum subsp. italicum, also known as cocklebur, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and seeds have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which can provide health benefits like relieving coughs, reducing fever, and promoting digestion. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these preparations and potential side effects of using this plant as a medicinal agent.
